user_query: I can't decide between the three options for Father's lunch on Day 1. Which one has the highest protein?

selected_nodes.name: Grilled Chicken Souvlaki Plate
selected_nodes.short_description: Marinated grilled chicken skewers served with fluffy couscous, a crisp cucumber-tomato salad, and creamy tzatziki — a classic Greek lunch plate.
selected_nodes.meal_suggestion: Marinate chicken cubes in olive oil, lemon juice, oregano, and garlic for at least 30 minutes. Thread onto skewers and grill until charred. Serve alongside fluffy couscous, a salad of diced cucumber, tomato, red onion, and kalamata olives, and a generous dollop of tzatziki. Add a lemon wedge and warm pita on the side.
selected_nodes.nutrition_note: Lean chicken breast is high in protein and low in saturated fat; couscous provides quick-digesting carbohydrates; cucumber and tomato add hydration, vitamins C and K; olives supply monounsaturated fats; tzatziki (yogurt-based) contributes calcium and probiotics. A balanced Mediterranean plate with protein, carbs, healthy fats, and vegetables.
selected_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Father > Day 1 > Lunch > Grilled Chicken Souvlaki Plate
selected_nodes.reasoning: Grilled Chicken Souvlaki Plate — uses lean grilled chicken breast, which is the highest-protein component among the three options (animal muscle protein concentration > legumes or cheese-based fillings).

selected_nodes.name: Mediterranean Lentil & Feta Salad
selected_nodes.short_description: A hearty salad of green lentils tossed with sun-dried tomatoes, feta, arugula, and a bright lemon-herb vinaigrette — plant-powered and filling.
selected_nodes.meal_suggestion: Cook green lentils until tender but not mushy; drain and cool slightly. Toss with chopped sun-dried tomatoes, crumbled feta, baby arugula, diced cucumber, and fresh dill. Dress with a vinaigrette of olive oil, lemon juice, Dijon mustard, and minced garlic. Serve at room temperature; keeps well for meal prep.
selected_nodes.nutrition_note: Lentils are an excellent source of plant-based protein, fiber, iron, and folate; sun-dried tomatoes concentrate lycopene and add umami; feta provides calcium and tangy flavor; arugula offers vitamins A, C, and K. Olive oil and lemon create a heart-healthy dressing. High in fiber for digestive health and blood sugar management.
selected_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Father > Day 1 > Lunch > Mediterranean Lentil & Feta Salad
selected_nodes.reasoning: Mediterranean Lentil & Feta Salad — lentils are an excellent plant-based protein source and the addition of feta increases protein, but overall typically provides less protein per serving than a chicken-based meal.

selected_nodes.name: Spanakopita-Style Stuffed Pita
selected_nodes.short_description: Warm pita pockets filled with a savory spinach-feta mixture inspired by Greek spanakopita — handheld, flavorful, and satisfying.
selected_nodes.meal_suggestion: Sauté fresh spinach with diced onion and garlic in olive oil until wilted. Mix with crumbled feta, a squeeze of lemon, dill, and a pinch of nutmeg. Stuff into warmed whole-wheat pita halves. Serve with a side of Greek salad or hummus and veggie sticks for a complete lunch.
selected_nodes.nutrition_note: Spinach is rich in iron, folate, and vitamins A and K; feta adds protein and calcium; whole-wheat pita provides fiber and complex carbohydrates. Olive oil contributes monounsaturated fats for heart health. A lighter take on traditional spanakopita without the phyllo pastry, reducing saturated fat while keeping all the classic flavors.
selected_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Father > Day 1 > Lunch > Spanakopita-Style Stuffed Pita
selected_nodes.reasoning: Spanakopita-Style Stuffed Pita — contains protein from feta and whole-wheat pita, but overall protein content is generally lower than the grilled chicken option and likely lower or similar to the lentil salad depending on portion sizes.
